prompt: A haunting, emotionally powerful single-frame art piece depicting preventable child mortality worldwide. Visual concept: An enormous aerial view looking down at a vast dark ocean at twilight. Thousands of tiny origami paper boats are scattered across the water — most are waterlogged, crumpled, and sinking beneath the surface in muted grey-blue tones, representing the 5 million children who still die each year from preventable causes. Near the bottom-right corner, a small cluster of paper boats in vivid warm colors (golden yellow, soft orange, hopeful coral) float intact on a patch of water illuminated by a single shaft of warm light breaking through heavy clouds above. The color gradient from dark desaturated mass to the small bright pocket tells the story: immense loss, fragile hope. The scale difference — thousands lost vs. the few in light — should be viscerally felt. No text, no labels, no charts, no axes. Photorealistic water with painterly/artistic paper boats. Cinematic lighting. The mood should evoke grief, urgency, and fragile hope simultaneously. Magazine-quality editorial photography aesthetic mixed with fine art sensibility. 4K quality, high resolution, emotionally devastating composition.
dataset_name: Child Mortality Estimates - UN Inter-agency Group for Child Mortality Estimation (UNICEF, WHO, World Bank, UN DESA)
insight: Nearly 5 million children under five still die each year from largely preventable causes, despite a 60% decline in global child mortality since 1990.
